definition:
1. dry land, land , ἐπὶ χέρσου, opposite to ἐν πόντῳ, (Odyssey by Homer); κύματα κυλινδόμενα προτὶ χέρσον (Odyssey by Homer); κῦμα χέρσῳ ῥηγνύμενον (Iliad by Homer); χέρσῳ on  or by land , (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
2. as adjective, χέρσος, ον, dry, firm , of land, (Herdotus Historicus); ἐν κονίᾳ χέρσῳ, opposite to πόντῳ, (Pindar) 
3. dry, hard, barren , (Herdotus Historicus); χ. λιμήν a harbour left dry , [variant datesAnthology Palantina 
4. metaphorically barren, without children , of women, (Sophocles Tragicus): with genitive barren of , πυρὰ χέρσος ἀγλαϊσμάτων (Euripides) 
Prob. from same Root as ξηρός. (ML)
